titles.title: qdiv: A Python package for microbial ecology analysis using the Hill number framework

institution.institution_name: Chalmers University of Technology, Department of Architecture and Civil Engineering, Division of Water Environment Technology, Gothenburg, Sweden

citation.article_title: Unifying species diversity, phylogenetic diversity, functional diversity, and related similarity and differentiation measures through hill numbers
citation.author: Chao
citation.journal_title: Annual Review of Ecology, Evolution, and Systematics
citation.unstructured_citation: Chao, A., Chiu, C.-H., & Jost, L. (2014). Unifying species diversity, phylogenetic diversity, functional diversity, and related similarity and differentiation measures through hill numbers. Annual Review of Ecology, Evolution, and Systematics, 45. https://doi.org/10.1146/annurev-ecolsys-120213-091540

citation.article_title: Diversity and evenness: A unifying notation and its consequences
citation.author: Hill
citation.unstructured_citation: Hill, M. O. (1973). Diversity and evenness: A unifying notation and its consequences. Ecology, 54(2), 427–432. https://doi.org/10.2307/1934352

citation.article_title: Partitioning diversity into independent alpha and beta components
citation.author: Jost
citation.unstructured_citation: Jost, L. (2007). Partitioning diversity into independent alpha and beta components. Ecology, 88(10), 2427–2439. https://doi.org/10.1890/06-1736.1

citation.article_title: Phylogenetic diversity measures and their decomposition: A framework based on hill numbers
citation.author: Chiu
citation.unstructured_citation: Chiu, C.-H., Jost, L., & Chao, A. (2014). Phylogenetic diversity measures and their decomposition: A framework based on hill numbers. Ecological Monographs, 84(1), 21–44. https://doi.org/10.1890/12-0960.1

citation.article_title: Distance-based functional diversity measures and their decomposition: A framework based on hill numbers
citation.author: Chiu
citation.unstructured_citation: Chiu, C.-H., & Chao, A. (2014). Distance-based functional diversity measures and their decomposition: A framework based on hill numbers. PLoS ONE, 9(7), e100014. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0100014

citation.author: Raup
citation.unstructured_citation: Raup, D. M., & Crick, R. E. (1979). Measurement of faunal similarity in paleontology. Journal of Paleontology, 53. https://www.jstor.org/stable/1304099
